items.title: Chelsea bench Fernández for Madrid comments
items.summary: Enzo Fernández will not play in either of Chelsea's next two matches after the club sanctioned him for comments made during the international break.

items.title: Liverpool's Slot: Isak training, not ready to start
items.summary: Arne Slot has said it is "definitely too fast" to expect Alexander Isak to feature in Liverpool's starting lineup following his return to team training but admitted it is a "very good thing" to have him as an option again.

items.title: Spurs boss De Zerbi sorry for Greenwood remarks
items.summary: Roberto De Zerbi has apologised to Tottenham Hotspur fans who were offended by his past comments about Mason Greenwood during his time as Marseille head coach.

items.title: Flick urges Rashford to step up for Barcelona
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said he gave Raphinha time off to clear his mind after he was left devastated by his recent injury, while urging Marcus Rashford to step up in the absence of the Brazil international.

items.title: Weekend predictions: Man City or Liverpool in FA Cup? Can Atlético play spoiler vs. Barça?
items.summary: From a big-club clash in England to a potentially decisive LaLiga matchup, another wild weekend of action awaits as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

items.title: Van Persie gaat in op blessure Sauer: 'Vooral voor Leo verschrikkelijk'
items.summary: Robin van Persie heeft vrijdag bevestigd dat Leo Sauer dit seizoen niet meer in actie komt voor Feyenoord. De Slowaakse buitenspeler startte de afgelopen interlandperiode tegen het advies van de Rotterdammers in in de basis bij de nationale ploeg en moest dat bekopen met een nieuwe blessure.

items.title: Lange blessureupdate AZ: Clasie keert terug, Koopmeiners valt weg
items.summary: Jordy Clasie keert tegen Fortuna Sittard na ruim zes maanden terug in de wedstrijdselectie van AZ. Daar staat wel negatief blessurenieuws tegenover, want Peer Koopmeiners ligt er even uit en voor Jizz Hornkamp is het einde seizoen.

items.title: Gattuso leaves Italy role - who could replace him?
items.summary: Manager Gennaro Gattuso and Italy agree to "mutually terminate" his contract after their failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup.

items.title: Hoefkens over paspoortgate: 'Bestaande regel, Ajax had zaken wél op orde'
items.summary: Carl Hoefkens (47) staat achter NAC Breda in paspoortgate . De trainer zag zijn werkgever een doos van Pandora openen, door een onderzoek bij de KNVB te eisen naar aanleiding van het meespelen van Dean James in de wedstrijd tegen Go Ahead Eagles (6-0). Daardoor stond de interlandperiode in het teken van werk- en verblijfsvergunningen bij de concurrerende clubs in de Eredivisie.

items.title: Championship clockwatch, no repercussions for Rodri’s Madrid talk: football news – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Rosenior: Fernández ‘crossed line’ with comments ⚽️ Live scores | Follow us on BlueSky | Email Simon Japan won last month’s Women’s Asian Cup in emphatic style: six wins out of six, 29 goals scored, one conceded. But yesterday they effectively sacked Nils Nielsen, the Greenlander who coached them to glory. Vanilla Ice was wrong: in football, it seems, sometimes even the best is a felony. Nielsen’s contract expired at the end of the tournament and the Japan Football Assocation decided not to offer him a new one. Norio Sasaki, the women’s national team director, said Japan could not win next year’s World Cup if Nielsen remained in charge, describing his coaching as “a little too lax, a little too soft”. Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea axe Enzo Fernández for two games after ‘crossing a line’ with Madrid comments
items.summary: Rosenior: ‘It’s disappointing for Enzo to speak that way’ ‘You have to protect this club and culture’ Enzo Fernández has been dropped for Chelsea’s next two games, with Liam Rosenior suggesting the club’s vice-captain had “crossed a line” in recent comments over his future. Following what he described as the worst 10 days of his career, Rosenior insisted he had to punish Fernández, who first questioned whether he would remain at Stamford Bridge beyond this season and then suggested he would like to live in Madrid – a comment widely viewed as courting a move to Real Madrid, a club with which he has frequently been linked. Continue reading...

items.title: Gennaro Gattuso exits as Italy manager after World Cup qualifying failure
items.summary: Italy have missed three consecutive World Cups Gattuso: ‘It has been an honour to lead national team’ Italy have parted company with their head coach, Gennaro Gattuso, the country’s football federation (FIGC) said on Friday, after the national team’s failure to qualify for the World Cup. Italy lost Tuesday’s playoff final in Bosnia-Herzegovina on penalties and the four-times champions have now missed out on three consecutive World Cups. Continue reading...

items.title: Telstar klaar voor eindfase: 'Zuiverder zijn, dan hoeven we ons geen zorgen te maken'
items.summary: Speler van de Maand Patrick Brouwer gaat dieper in op de eindfase van de competitie. De aanvaller van Telstar heeft er na de knappe zege op PSV alle vertrouwen in dat zijn ploeg zich gaat handhaven. Verslaggever Jort van der Hoorn zocht Brouwer op in Velsen-Zuid.

items.title: Bosz gaat in op VI-artikel over achilleshiel PSV: 'Daar trainen wij op'
items.summary: Van alle clubs uit de Eredivisie heeft PSV de meeste moeite met het verdedigen van lange inworpen. Dat is bekend bij Peter Bosz, die hier met zijn selectie op traint.

items.title: Isak could make injury return against Man City
items.summary: Alexander Isak could feature in Liverpool's FA Cup quarter-final against Manchester City after missing more than three months through injury.

items.title: Chelsea's Fernandez dropped after 'crossing a line'
items.summary: Chelsea's Enzo Fernandez will sit out their next two games after he "crossed a line", says manager Liam Rosenior.

items.title: Hurtling towards relegation - but can Port Vale shock Chelsea in FA Cup?
items.summary: Six thousand Port Vale fans will travel to Chelsea on Saturday to cheer on the struggling League One side in the quarter-finals of the FA Cup.

items.title: What De Zerbi needs to do to turn Tottenham around
items.summary: Tony Pulis explores why Tottenham are in such a mess and what their new manager Roberto de Zerbi might do to change things as they seek to stay in the Premier League.

items.title: Matthieu Epolo absent, et quatre joueurs écartés du noyau A du Standard avant le déplacement à Louvain !
items.summary: Vincent Euvrard a livré plusieurs nouvelles importantes en conférence de presse. Matthieu Epolo ne défendra pas les cages du Standard en déplacement à Louvain, et quatre joueurs ont été écartés du noyau A !

items.title: Bloedbad bij Italië lijkt nog niet voorbij: ook kop van bondscoach Gattuso rolt
items.summary: De nieuwe WK-misser van Italië heeft zware gevolgen binnen de voetbalbond. Na de uitschakeling tegen Bosnië en Herzegovina is nu ook bondscoach Gennaro Gattuso ontslagen.

items.title: Officiel : Gennaro Gattuso paie l'échec de l'Italie, deux noms font déjà grand bruit pour lui succéder
items.summary: Gennaro Gattuso n'est plus le sélectionneur de l'Italie. Il s'en va le coeur lourd après la non-qualification pour la Coupe du Monde.

items.title: Un huitième et puis c'est tout ? Voici les probabilités de voir les Diables remporter la Coupe du Monde
items.summary: Les Diables Rouges ont soufflé le chaud et le froid pour le premier rassemblement de 2026. La Belgique est loin d'être favorite de la Coupe du Monde qui approche.

items.title: Throwback ... friday? Slechte herinnering voor STVV kan hét geluk worden voor KRC Genk
items.summary: De Champions' Play-offs staan op beginnen. Mogelijk is het de laatste keer dat we play-offs hebben in ons land. STVV mag als nummer drie mikken op Europees voetbal, maar zal ook nog wel wat herinneringen hebben aan dat eerste seizoen met play-offs.

items.title: Casper Nielsen : "Le sixième des Play-Offs 1 ne gagne rien. Le Standard peut encore gagner quelque chose"
items.summary: De retour après sa blessure au coude, Casper Nielsen entend montrer une mentalité irréprochable en Europe Play-Offs et espère que tous ses coéquipiers en feront de même. Un passage inévitable pour le Standard, qui veut encore rêver d'un ticket européen.

items.title: Leuk voor Nathan De Cat maar minder leuk voor Anderlecht? "Hij is heel moe teruggekeerd"
items.summary: Als Rode Duivel op amper 17-jarige leeftijd maakt Nathan De Cat Anderlecht trots. De jonge middenvelder keerde wel erg vermoeid terug uit de Verenigde Staten, zo vertelt ploegmaat Marco Kana.

items.title: Les dernières rumeurs, les officialisations : suivez le mercato en direct ! 03/04: Matondo
items.summary: Toutes les rumeurs de transfert, toutes les officialisations regroupées en un seul article. On vous dit tout sur le mercato en Jupiler Pro League, en Challenger Pro League et sur celui des Diables Rouges... en direct !

items.title: Zorgt deze club voor weinig spanning in de Europe Play-offs? "Ik zie zelfs geen enkele uitdager"
items.summary: KRC Genk begint vrijdag als koploper aan de Europe Play-offs en lijkt voor velen ook de uitgesproken favoriet voor het laatste Europese ticket. Jacky Mathijssen en Steven Defour lieten zich bij HBvL uit over de situatie.

items.title: Vlak voor start play-offs krijgt Zulte Waregem twee stevige tikken te verwerken
items.summary: Zulte Waregem begint zaterdag als leider aan de Relegation Play-offs, maar krijgt vlak voor de clash met Cercle Brugge ook twee stevige tegenvallers te verwerken in de kern.

items.title: FA Cup quarter-finals: things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Chelsea dare not lose, West Ham and Leeds play out a survival dress rehearsal, while Phil Foden urgently needs to make an impact Phil Foden made two starts for England over the international break as Thomas Tuchel experimented with how the Manchester City attacking midfielder could be used at the World Cup. He played in a couple of positions but was ineffective in two collectively subpar performances from the Three Lions. It means he has one assist and no goals in his past 22 appearances for club and country in what has been an underwhelming campaign for the 25-year-old. He has dropped below Rayan Cherki in Pep Guardiola’s pecking order and has not completed a full 90 minutes since January. In the Carabao Cup final, Foden was permitted a late cameo, and it feels as if this is the time when he should be making a difference in the final stages of the season. There are still trophies to be played for, even if winning the Premier League title would involve a huge turnaround against Arsenal. This means the FA Cup is the most promising prospect and Foden should be desperate to start and remind everyone of the world-class player he could be, especially against a Liverpool side who often struggle against smart No 10s. The next six weeks could be make or break for Foden’s City and international career, so he must seize every opportunity. WU Manchester City v Liverpool, Saturday 12.45pm (all times BST) Chelsea v Port Vale, Saturday 5.15pm Southampton v Arsenal, Saturday 8pm West Ham v Leeds, Sunday 4.30pm Continue reading...

items.title: Tottenham head coach Roberto De Zerbi apologises for past comments on Mason Greenwood
items.summary: ‘I didn’t meant to take a stance,’ says Spurs head coach Fan groups had opposed De Zerbi’s appointment The new Tottenham head coach, Roberto De Zerbi, has issued an apology for his past comments on Mason Greenwood and said he would never intentionally downplay violence against women. The Tottenham Hotspur Supporters’ Trust (THST) strongly criticised the Italian’s appointment on Tuesday, releasing a statement that expressed “serious and far-reaching concerns” soon after he was confirmed to have signed a five-year deal in replacing the interim manager Igor Tudor. Continue reading...
